description: Risk of severe bleeding. Aspirin increases the anti-platelet effect while Warfarin increases anticoagulation. Combined use significantly raises the risk of gastrointestinal and internal bleeding.
Risk of serotonin syndrome. Combining SSRIs with MAOIs can lead to dangerously high levels of serotonin, causing confusion, rapid heart rate, and potentially fatal complications.
NSAIDs like ibuprofen can reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors like lisinopril and increase the risk of renal impairment.
Amiodarone increases the blood level of simvastatin, which may increase the risk of muscle damage (rhabdomyolysis).
Furosemide can cause electrolyte imbalances (hypokalemia) which increases the toxicity of digoxin, potentially leading to arrhythmias.
Both medications can increase potassium levels. Combined use may lead to hyperkalemia, especially in patients with renal impairment.
Contraindicated. Co-administration can cause severe and potentially fatal hypotension (dangerously low blood pressure).
